commands.review_summary:
The PR raises the shared small-context minimum prompt-budget ratio from 50% to 87.5% and adds a regression test for a 4,096-token lightweight cron prompt.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +19. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main leaves only 2,048 prompt tokens for the reported 4,096-token context and 20,000-token reserve, so the 3,544-token prompt still overflows into `compact_only`. I did not run a live cron job in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers are real behavior proof and acceptance of the shared small-context budget tradeoff, not a narrow automation repair.

target.title: fix(agents): keep lightweight cron prompts out of no-op compaction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96658.

Lightweight isolated cron runs on very small context windows can still reserve half the model context for response headroom, leaving too little room for the rendered prompt. In the reported 4,096-token model case, current main leaves only 2,048 prompt tokens for a 3,544-token lightweight cron prompt, so the precheck routes to `compact_only` even though lightweight cron has no bootstrap context to compact.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the existing absolute 8k prompt-budget floor for larger models, but raises the small-context prompt share from 50% to 87.5%. That leaves enough prompt room on 4k local models to submit lightweight cron prompts instead of entering no-op compaction recovery.

A focused regression test covers the reported boundary: 4,096 context tokens, 20,000 configured reserve tokens, 3,544 rendered prompt tokens, no history, and no reducible tool results.

## User Impact

Lightweight cron jobs on small-context fallback models can proceed when the rendered prompt fits the model window, instead of failing through repeated compact-only recovery with nothing useful to compact. Larger-context behavior still uses the 8k absolute prompt-budget floor.

## Evidence

Red test before the fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts
AssertionError: expected 2048 to be greater than or equal to 3544
```

Patched helper behavior proof for the reported 4,096-token / 3,544-token lightweight cron boundary:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module - <<'EOF_NODE'
import { shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts';

const result = shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt({
  contextTokenBudget: 4096,
  reserveTokens: 20000,
  currentHistoryTokens: 0,
  llmBoundaryTokenPressure: {
    estimatedPromptTokens: 3544,
    toolResultReducibleChars: 0,
  },
});

console.log(JSON.stringify({
  route: result.route,
  shouldCompact: result.shouldCompact,
  estimatedPromptTokens: result.estimatedPromptTokens,
  promptBudgetBeforeReserve: result.promptBudgetBeforeReserve,
  overflowTokens: result.overflowTokens,
  effectiveReserveTokens: result.effectiveReserveTokens,
  toolResultReducibleChars: result.toolResultReducibleChars,
}, null, 2));
EOF_NODE
```

```json
{
  "route": "fits",
  "shouldCompact": false,
  "estimatedPromptTokens": 3544,
  "promptBudgetBeforeReserve": 3584,
  "overflowTokens": 0,
  "effectiveReserveTokens": 512,
  "toolResultReducibleChars": 0
}
```

Focused validation after the fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-settings.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-compaction-constants.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Live PR checks: `python3 /Users/fallmonkey/.codex/plugins/cache/openai-curated-remote/github/0.1.5/skills/gh-fix-ci/scripts/inspect_pr_checks.py --repo . --pr 96668 --json` reported no failing checks. The current check rollup includes successful `Real behavior proof`, `runner-admission`, `actionlint`, `no-tabs`, and label checks; skipped matrix jobs are from path/runner gating, and there are no actionable failures.

Review comments: no unresolved review threads were present when checked with GitHub GraphQL. The only PR comment was the ClawSweeper "review started" placeholder, with no actionable findings yet.

Autoreview: attempted one local draft-PR review round, but the helper failed before review due local Codex config parsing (`service_tier = priority`; helper expected `fast` or `flex`). No review findings were produced.

Agent transcript: a matching local session was found, but no transcript is included because adding sanitized transcript logs requires explicit approval.
